item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for your idea! I considered this when I was originally making the article. However, I realized this goes against the basic nature of the foundation. The foundation prioritizes containment over research, and in this case, they have it completely contained. This seemed more like something the Chaos Insurgency would do: weaponize a skip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To me it's a very original idea thogh I would love more experiments like having a D class look at it and see a picture of a POI I understand it would be complicated to add.

+1 from me for that ending

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You are entirely correct. I have gone through and revised my use of pronouns.
Thank you for your comment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Referring to everyone involved as "they" was not only pointless, but became a little confusing when describing a fight between two of the individuals.

The concept is an interesting one, though, with a clear artistic message appropriate for AWCY?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was kind of on the fence about this but I actually did not see the ending of the interviee coming. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you. I found and fixed the issue.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You forgot to change the "SCP-XXXX" to SCP-4124 in the paragraph after the interview. Also, the redactions and expungements are usually in all caps.
Its interesting though. I enjoyed reading it +1

item.title: Minor suggestion
item.content:encoded: Four minutes seems like a long time for silence. It pulled me out of the immersion thinking about them sitting there for four whole minutes saying nothing.
